text: vv. 1-11. According to the opinion of St Jerome, the events here described happened only in a vision.
The Latin verb has rather the sense of ‘scatter’, but this loses the idea of the parable.
There can be no certainty which king is alluded to; if it was Joakim or Joachin, the ‘cities of the south’ may perhaps be the border-cities of Egypt (cf. IV Kg. 24.7).

In the Latin version, the new-comers from the north are addressed; the Hebrew text, which is evidently faulty, gives, ‘Lift thou your eyes, and see thou the men coming from the north’. The connexion between the two halves of the verse is in any case obscure, and there may have been an omission. The word ‘Jerusalem’ is not expressed in the original until verse 27.
